subject: Sociology
title: White Privilege
description: A quick win: understand White Privilege.

hook.text: He's never been followed in a store, asked to speak for his race, or had his name mispronounced as a joke.

buildup.text: Peggy McIntosh described white privilege as an invisible knapsack of unearned advantages carried through daily life.

discovery.text: Privilege doesn't mean life is easy. It means race isn't one of the things making it harder.

twist.text: Privilege is invisible to those who have it. That invisibility is itself part of how privilege functions.

climax.text: Acknowledging privilege isn't guilt. It's the starting point for understanding how systems distribute advantage.

punchline.text: Privilege is not what you earned. It's what you never had to.

quiz.question: What did Peggy McIntosh describe white privilege as?
options: An invisible knapsack of unearned advantages
A financial bonus given by the government
A theory with no real-world evidence
